content: After applying the correction for elevation and temperature, the runway length is 700 m . The corrected runway length (in m ) for an effective gradient of $1 \%$ is __________ (round off to the nearest integer).
explanation:
Given:

Corrected length after applying elevation and temperature correction,

$$ \begin{aligned} l & =700 \mathrm{~m} \\ \text { Gradient } & =1 \% \end{aligned} $$

For every 1\% gradient, runway length increase by 20\%

$$ \begin{aligned} \text { Therefore corrected runway length } & =700+700 \times \frac{20}{100} \\ = & 700 \times 1.2=840 \mathrm{~m} \end{aligned} $$

content: The maximum degree of the curve that can be used for railways in a mountainous region is
explanation: The maximum degree of the curve that can be used for railways in a mountainous region is $40^{\circ}$.

content: The base length of the runway at the mean sea level (MSL) is 1500 m. If the runway is located at an altitude of 300 m above the MSL, the actual length (in m) of the runway to be provided is _________. (round off to the nearest integer)
explanation:
The actual length of the runway required at an altitude of 300 m can be calculated using a correction factor for the altitude. The correction factor for altitude is typically 7% increase in runway length for every 300 m rise in altitude above mean sea level.

Let's denote :

$ L_0 $ as the base length of the runway at mean sea level (MSL)

$ h $ as the altitude above MSL

$ L $ as the actual length of the runway required at altitude $ h $

Given :

$ L_0 = 1500 $ m

$ h = 300 $ m

The increase in runway length can be calculated as :

$$ L = L_0 \times \left(1 + \frac{h}{300} \times 0.07 \right) $$

Substitute the given values :

$$ L = 1500 \times \left(1 + \frac{300}{300} \times 0.07 \right) $$

Simplify the expression :

$$ L = 1500 \times (1 + 0.07) $$

$$ L = 1500 \times 1.07 $$

$$ L = 1605 \, \text{m} $$

Therefore, the actual length of the runway to be provided at an altitude of 300 m is 1605 meters. Rounding off to the nearest integer, the length is 1605 meters.
